wiki.summary: "Love Me Not" is a song by singer-songwriter Ravyn Lenae, released on May 3, 2024 as the joint lead single alongside Love Is Blind to her second album Bird's Eye. Love Me Not is described as an R&B, "soul-rock", indie rock, grungy & "retro pop" song. The song finds Lenae considering her romantic desires and whether to commit to a relationship that is currently unstable. She explores the duality of needing her partner and also wanting to be independent. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "Love Me Not" is a song by singer-songwriter Ravyn Lenae, released on May 3, 2024 as the joint lead single alongside Love Is Blind to her second album Bird's Eye. Love Me Not is described as an R&B, "soul-rock", indie rock, grungy & "retro pop" song. The song finds Lenae considering her romantic desires and whether to commit to a relationship that is currently unstable. She explores the duality of needing her partner and also wanting to be independent. In the chorus, Ravyn sings with dreamy vocals on top of drums, "Oh no, I don't need you, but I miss you, come here". Love Me Not rose in popularity on TikTok when a user released a mashup of the song & Solange's Losing You on October 11, 2024. The song slowly gained momentum on TikTok for the rest of 2024, with the increasing use of the mashup in videos, and gained much more recognition by January 2025. Love Me Not debuted at #81 on the Billboard Hot 100 on the week of April 12, 2025, & reached the top 10 for the first time on the week of July 26, 2025, rising from #12 to #7 & becoming Ravyn's first top 10 single. Love Me Not soon peaked at #5 for a week in August 2025, marking her first top-5. Love Me Not ranked at #30 on Billboard's Year End list of 2025. Read more on Last.fm.
